{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/53","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/53/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/53/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/53/events","html_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/issues/53","id":2429274639,"node_id":"I_kwDOBDfbCs6Qy8oP","number":53,"title":"Document the version of NumPy used to build the packages","user":{"login":"kinow","id":304786,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDc4Ng==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/304786?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow","html_url":"https://github.com/kinow","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2024-07-25T07:46:20Z","updated_at":"2024-08-03T11:15:12Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hello,

We have been using Crick with NumPy 1.x in our workflows, and it worked well so far. However, we recently upgraded our containers and after the build, running our code resulted in errors like (sorry the trimmed traceback, the code is not open source yet),

```bash
    from crick import TDigest
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/crick/__init__.py", line 4, in <module>
    from .space_saving import SpaceSaving
  File "crick/space_saving.pyx", line 1, in init crick.space_saving
ValueError: numpy.dtype size changed, may indicate binary incompatibility. Expected 96 from C header, got 88 from PyObject
```

To solve this, we replaced the `crick=0.0.5` dependency in our `setup.py` and used `git+https://github.com/dask/crick.git@0.0.5` instead. Out guess was that that would build from sources, using our installed NumPy 2.0.1 dependecy. That worked, and our workflow was executed successfully using crick 0.0.5 + numpy 2.0.1.

We couldn't find the information about which version of NumPy was used with Crick 0.0.5, thus this issue :slightly_smiling_face: 

Cheers

EDIT: We are not discarding that maybe another dependency is causing that, but the only difference we could see from our previous container was the newer NumPy version being brought in by another dependency that uses it.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/53/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/53/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/comments/2266679730","html_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/issues/53#issuecomment-2266679730","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/53","id":2266679730,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBDfbCs6HGsmy","user":{"login":"kinow","id":304786,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDc4Ng==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/304786?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow","html_url":"https://github.com/kinow","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-03T11:15:12Z","updated_at":"2024-08-03T11:15:12Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> The conda package for crick now supports numpy 2: [conda-forge/crick-feedstock#32](https://github.com/conda-forge/crick-feedstock/pull/32)

Great news! I wasn't aware of the conda package. I believe so far we have used `pip install`, executed using the system `python3` (from ubuntu 22.04 base image). So for now we will have to keep the option to install from sources, I believe. We have been using Crick with NumPy 1.x in our workflows, and it worked well so far. However, we recently upgraded our containers and after the build, running our code resulted in errors like (sorry the trimmed traceback, the code is not open source yet),

```bash
    from crick import TDigest
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/crick/__init__.py", line 4, in <module>
    from .space_saving import SpaceSaving
  File "crick/space_saving.pyx", line 1, in init crick.space_saving
ValueError: numpy.dtype size changed, may indicate binary incompatibility. Expected 96 from C header, got 88 from PyObject
```

To solve this, we replaced the `crick=0.0.5` dependency in our `setup.py` and used `git+https://github.com/dask/crick.git@0.0.5` instead. Out guess was that that would build from sources, using our installed NumPy 2.0.1 dependecy. That worked, and our workflow was executed successfully using crick 0.0.5 + numpy 2.0.1.

We couldn't find the information about which version of NumPy was used with Crick 0.0.5, thus this issue :slightly_smiling_face: 

Cheers

EDIT: We are not discarding that maybe another dependency is causing that, but the only difference we could see from our previous container was the newer NumPy version being brought in by another dependency that uses it.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/53/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/53/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/comments/2297387128","html_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/issues/53#issuecomment-2297387128","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/53","id":2297387128,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBDfbCs6I71h4","user":{"login":"kinow","id":304786,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDc4Ng==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/304786?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow","html_url":"https://github.com/kinow","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-19T20:24:52Z","updated_at":"2024-08-19T20:24:52Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> Proposing we rebuild with `numpy=2` over in #55 for those who are interested

That's great! Out of curiosity, is there any flag/setting/indicator anywhere in the downloaded file from crick that shows which version of numpy was actually used? This package is used in an important part of our workflow, and I'd like to document it internally, so that if there are other issues like what we had, there is a step to verify the container's numpy version against the version used to build crick (even though I think any 2.x numpy should be compatible.... but just in case).

{"action":"opened","number":59,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/pulls/59","id":2035982046,"node_id":"PR_kwDOBDfbCs55Wp7e","html_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/pull/59","diff_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/pull/59.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/pull/59.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/59","number":59,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"GH-53: Export the version of NumPy used to build the project as a constant (via setuptools/cython preprocessor injecting the value during build)","user":{"login":"kinow","id":304786,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDc4Ng==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/304786?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow","html_url":"https://github.com/kinow","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Closes #53 

Used the approach found [on this SO](https://stackoverflow.com/a/48411593). It basically injects `np.__version__` during build time and defines as a constant in C/Python, available via a function imported in `__init__`. Then we export `__numpy_version__` in `__init__.py`.

Tested by installing with `pip install -e .` which used `numpy=2.1.0`. Then created a new venv, and installed this version again, but installed also `numpy=2.0`.

```bash
(venv2) kinow@ranma:~/Development/python/workspace/crick$ pip install numpy==2.0
Collecting numpy==2.0
  Using cached numpy-2.0.0-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl.metadata (60 kB)
Using cached numpy-2.0.0-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl (19.0 MB)
Installing collected packages: numpy
  Attempting uninstall: numpy
    Found existing installation: numpy 2.1.0
    Uninstalling numpy-2.1.0:
      Successfully uninstalled numpy-2.1.0
Successfully installed numpy-2.0.0
(venv2) kinow@ranma:~/Development/python/workspace/crick$ python -c 'from crick import __numpy_version__; print(__numpy_version__)'
b'2.1.0'
(venv2) kinow@ranma:~/Development/python/workspace/crick$ pip list | grep numpy
numpy   2.0.0
```

(above it prints `__numpy_version__` as 2.1.0 as that's the build-time version, and then `numpy 2.0.0` as that's the version my venv is using)

Not sure if the most elegant way… tried different approaches, but I couldn't export/inject the `np.__version__`, as there was only a `NPY_VERSION` in C NumPy, but didn't appear to match the complete NumPy version (only major?, but also couldn't export that).

Added a simple unit test, that could in theory test `__numpy_version__ == np.__version__`, but I am not sure if that will be always true in CICD. Happy to change that. Also fixed a couple of typos I saw while changing the code, also happy to drop those if better.

Cheers,
Bruno","created_at":"2024-08-25T12:22:00Z","updated_at":"2024-08-25T12:22:00Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":null,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/pulls/59/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/pulls/59/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/59/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/statuses/375643ca1f1d971ca4da269d71a2a7c6c12a3a9b","head":{"label":"kinow:gh-53-export-numpy-version","ref":"gh-53-export-numpy-version","sha":"375643ca1f1d971ca4da269d71a2a7c6c12a3a9b","user":{"login":"kinow","id":304786,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDc4Ng==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/304786?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow","html_url":"https://github.com/kinow","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":847291933,"node_id":"R_kgDOMoCmHQ","name":"crick","full_name":"kinow/crick","private":false,"owner":{"login":"kinow","id":304786,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDc4Ng==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/304786?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow","html_u We have been using Crick with NumPy 1.x in our workflows, and it worked well so far. However, we recently upgraded our containers and after the build, running our code resulted in errors like (sorry the trimmed traceback, the code is not open source yet),

```bash
    from crick import TDigest
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/crick/__init__.py", line 4, in <module>
    from .space_saving import SpaceSaving
  File "crick/space_saving.pyx", line 1, in init crick.space_saving
ValueError: numpy.dtype size changed, may indicate binary incompatibility. Expected 96 from C header, got 88 from PyObject
```

To solve this, we replaced the `crick=0.0.5` dependency in our `setup.py` and used `git+https://github.com/dask/crick.git@0.0.5` instead. Out guess was that that would build from sources, using our installed NumPy 2.0.1 dependecy. That worked, and our workflow was executed successfully using crick 0.0.5 + numpy 2.0.1.

We couldn't find the information about which version of NumPy was used with Crick 0.0.5, thus this issue :slightly_smiling_face: 

Cheers

EDIT: We are not discarding that maybe another dependency is causing that, but the only difference we could see from our previous container was the newer NumPy version being brought in by another dependency that uses Export the version of NumPy used to build the project as a constant (via setuptools/cython preprocessor injecting the value during build)","user":{"login":"kinow","id":304786,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDc4Ng==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/304786?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow","html_url":"https://github.com/kinow","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-08-25T12:22:00Z","updated_at":"2024-08-26T09:52:47Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/pulls/59","html_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/pull/59","diff_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/pull/59.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/pull/59.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Closes #53 

Used the approach found [on this SO](https://stackoverflow.com/a/48411593). It basically injects `np.__version__` during build time and defines as a constant in C/Python, available via a function imported in `__init__`. Then we export `__numpy_version__` in `__init__.py`.

Tested by installing with `pip install -e .` which used `numpy=2.1.0`. Then created a new venv, and installed this version again, but installed also `numpy=2.0`.

```bash
(venv2) kinow@ranma:~/Development/python/workspace/crick$ pip install numpy==2.0
Collecting numpy==2.0
  Using cached numpy-2.0.0-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl.metadata (60 kB)
Using cached numpy-2.0.0-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl (19.0 MB)
Installing collected packages: numpy
  Attempting uninstall: numpy
    Found existing installation: numpy 2.1.0
    Uninstalling numpy-2.1.0:
      Successfully uninstalled numpy-2.1.0
Successfully installed numpy-2.0.0
(venv2) kinow@ranma:~/Development/python/workspace/crick$ python -c 'from crick import __numpy_version__; print(__numpy_version__)'
b'2.1.0'
(venv2) kinow@ranma:~/Development/python/workspace/crick$ pip list | grep numpy
numpy   2.0.0
```

(above it prints `__numpy_version__` as 2.1.0 as that's the build-time version, and then `numpy 2.0.0` as that's the version my venv is using)

Not sure if the most elegant way… tried different approaches, but I couldn't export/inject the `np.__version__`, as there was only a `NPY_VERSION` in C NumPy, but didn't appear to match the complete NumPy version (only major?, but also couldn't export that).

Added a simple unit test, that could in theory test `__numpy_version__ == np.__version__`, but I am not sure if that will be always true in CICD. Happy to change that. Also fixed a couple of typos I saw while changing the code, also happy to drop those if better.

Cheers,
Bruno","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/59/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/59/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/comments/2309812952","html_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/pull/59#issuecomment-2309812952","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/59","id":2309812952,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBDfbCs6JrPLY","user":{"login":"kinow","id":304786,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDc4Ng==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/304786?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow","html_url":"https://github.com/kinow","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-26T09:52:46Z","updated_at":"2024-08-26T09:52:46Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> looks like pre-commit isn't happy

build)","user":{"login":"kinow","id":304786,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDc4Ng==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/304786?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow","html_url":"https://github.com/kinow","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-08-25T12:22:00Z","updated_at":"2024-08-26T12:01:27Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/pulls/59","html_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/pull/59","diff_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/pull/59.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/pull/59.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Closes #53 

Used the approach found [on this SO](https://stackoverflow.com/a/48411593). It basically injects `np.__version__` during build time and defines as a constant in C/Python, available via a function imported in `__init__`. Then we export `__numpy_version__` in `__init__.py`.

Tested by installing with `pip install -e .` which used `numpy=2.1.0`. Then created a new venv, and installed this version again, but installed also `numpy=2.0`.

```bash
(venv2) kinow@ranma:~/Development/python/workspace/crick$ pip install numpy==2.0
Collecting numpy==2.0
  Using cached numpy-2.0.0-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl.metadata (60 kB)
Using cached numpy-2.0.0-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl (19.0 MB)
Installing collected packages: numpy
  Attempting uninstall: numpy
    Found existing installation: numpy 2.1.0
    Uninstalling numpy-2.1.0:
      Successfully uninstalled numpy-2.1.0
Successfully installed numpy-2.0.0
(venv2) kinow@ranma:~/Development/python/workspace/crick$ python -c 'from crick import __numpy_version__; print(__numpy_version__)'
b'2.1.0'
(venv2) kinow@ranma:~/Development/python/workspace/crick$ pip list | grep numpy
numpy   2.0.0
```

(above it prints `__numpy_version__` as 2.1.0 as that's the build-time version, and then `numpy 2.0.0` as that's the version my venv is using)

Not sure if the most elegant way… tried different approaches, but I couldn't export/inject the `np.__version__`, as there was only a `NPY_VERSION` in C NumPy, but didn't appear to match the complete NumPy version (only major?, but also couldn't export that).

Added a simple unit test, that could in theory test `__numpy_version__ == np.__version__`, but I am not sure if that will be always true in CICD. Happy to change that. Also fixed a couple of typos I saw while changing the code, also happy to drop those if better.

Cheers,
Bruno","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/59/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/59/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/comments/2310037383","html_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/pull/59#issuecomment-2310037383","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/59","id":2310037383,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBDfbCs6JsF-H","user":{"login":"kinow","id":304786,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDc4Ng==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/304786?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow","html_url":"https://github.com/kinow","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-26T12:01:26Z","updated_at":"2024-08-26T12:01:26Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Applied linter proposed fixes manually, I think the workflow is waiting approval now (first time contributor here). We have been using Crick with NumPy 1.x in our workflows, and it worked well so far. However, we recently upgraded our containers and after the build, running our code resulted in errors like (sorry the trimmed traceback, the code is not open source yet),

```bash
    from crick import TDigest
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/crick/__init__.py", line 4, in <module>
    from .space_saving import SpaceSaving
  File "crick/space_saving.pyx", line 1, in init crick.space_saving
ValueError: numpy.dtype size changed, may indicate binary incompatibility. Expected 96 from C header, got 88 from PyObject
```

To solve this, we replaced the `crick=0.0.5` dependency in our `setup.py` and used `git+https://github.com/dask/crick.git@0.0.5` instead. Out guess was that that would build from sources, using our installed NumPy 2.0.1 dependecy. That worked, and our workflow was executed successfully using crick 0.0.5 + numpy 2.0.1.

We couldn't find the information about which version of NumPy was used with Crick 0.0.5, thus this issue :slightly_smiling_face: 

Cheers","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/53/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/53/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/42","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/42/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/42/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/crick/issues/42/events","html_url":"https://github.com/dask/crick/issues/42","id":2040088492,"node_id":"I_kwDOBDfbCs55mUes","number":42,"title":"crick fails to install with numpy >= 1.26","user":{"login":"kinow","id":304786,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDc4Ng==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/304786?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow","html_url":"https://github.com/kinow","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kinow/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-12-13T16:36:45Z","updated_at":"2023-12-13T16:36:45Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the issue**:

Creating a mamba env that contains simply `numpy` , and with `crick==v0.0.4` as a dependency fails with: `No module named 'numpy.distutils'`

**Minimal Complete Verifiable Example**:

```yaml
name: my_env
channels:
  - conda-forge
dependencies:
  - python
  - numpy
  - xarray
  - dask
  - zarr
  .......
  - pip
  - pip:
    - -e .[all]
```

And in `setup.py` we have:

```py
    install_requires=[
     ...........
        "crick==0.0.4",
    ],

```

**Anything else we need to know?**:

We fixed it by pinning Numpy to <1.26. The Numpy 1.26 notes explain about the distutils replacement/drop: https://github.com/numpy/numpy/releases/tag/v1.26.0

Here's a commit where they updated example code that used the `get_info` function used in `crick`'s `setup.py`.

https://github.com/numpy/numpy/commit/8c0e2bc4746417269c9cca105c729a08a0349a7e

**Environment**:

- Dask version: 2023.12.0
- Python version: 3.11.6
- Operating System: Ubuntu LTS
- Install method (conda, pip, source): Conda + Pip

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Closes https://github.com/common-workflow-language/cwl-utils/issues/138

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

+{% set name = "cwl-utils" %}
+{% set version = "0.14" %}
+
+package:

+
+source:
+  url: https://pypi.io/packages/source/{{ name[0] }}/{{ name }}/cwl-utils-{{ version }}.tar.gz
+  sha256: 4ef2834aec167523c6079f78e42020aafbc88a697ca77449a3f85a6ddd243095
+
+build:
+  # Copied from schema-salad
+  skip: true  # [py2k or win]
+  script: {{ PYTHON }} -m pip install . -vv
+  number: 0
+
+requirements:
+  host:
+    - pip
+    - python
+  run:
+    - cwl-upgrader >=1.2
+    - packaging
+    - python
+    - requests
+    - schema-salad >=8.2,<9
+
+test:
+  imports:
+    - cwl_utils
+  commands:
+    - pip check

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".

- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.

- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".

- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Closes https://github.com/common-workflow-language/cwl-utils/issues/138

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".

- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

+{% set name = "cwl-utils" %}
+{% set version = "0.18" %}
+
+package:

+
+source:
+  url: https://pypi.io/packages/source/{{ name[0] }}/{{ name }}/cwl-utils-{{ version }}.tar.gz

+
+build:

+  script: {{ PYTHON }} -m pip install . -vv

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Closes https://github.com/common-workflow-language/cwl-utils/issues/138

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".

- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Closes https://github.com/common-workflow-language/cwl-utils/issues/138

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Closes https://github.com/common-workflow-language/cwl-utils/issues/138

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable

Two commits here, but happy to squash if necessary. Found a typo in the docs for new contributors (double "like" in <https://pydata-sphinx-theme.readthedocs.io/en/stable/contribute/manual.html>).

So I ran the PyCharm linter, and added the other typos found in code comments.

The other commit is for `logging.warn`, which was deprecated in [3.3](https://bugs.python.org/issue13235) and is to be removed some day :+1: 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/304786/177641333-08e70a81-1dbe-4f82-aa15-4887f7836095.png)

Thanks!

The search is useful (although I wouldn't mind if it was moved to the top bar as mentioned in linked discussions), but having [our table of contents](https://github.com/common-workflow-language/user_guide/issues/222) rendered on the left-sidebar would be better for us. For now, I found the simplest way to achieve this—after looking at @choldgraf's example in #536 (thanks!)—to just pass the `startdepth=0` in our sidebar HTML (in `_templates/sidebar-nav-bs.html`). We were already using `sidebar-nav-bs.html` to customize the Bootstrap CSS classes to hide the sidebar in small/mobile mode, related to #43. ```
{{ generate_nav_html("sidebar", startdepth=0, show_nav_level=theme_show_nav_level|int, maxdepth=theme_navigation_depth|int, collapse=theme_collapse_navigation|tobool, includehidden=True, titles_only=True) }}
``` ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/304786/178397008-e0648807-d76a-43c8-a055-d39d0ba8a166.png) No matter the level the user is reading, the sidebar now displays the complete TOC from level `0`, which so far looks good to us. But I believe others could try to customize it a bit if they prefer to have a different sidebar for the landing page, and for the other pages. ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/304786/178396935-d0b4df53-6d72-48fb-b071-86600a4b0545.png) Thanks! -Bruno ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/pydata/pydata-sphinx-theme/issues/comments/1181250562/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

pray Sure! >> State of the field: Do the authors describe how this software compares to other commonly-used packages? > >In the paper draft I believe you mention that while the techniques used in helayo is similar to those used in the bioinformatics field, but very different to modern bioinformatics techniques that are too specific to be used in natural language processing. > >But reading the paper I had a few questions, like “could I use it for Chinese and Japanese manuscripts?”, I believe they suffer similar issue to what you described in the motivation section of the paper. Some papers include a section about future work, improvements, etc, not sure if necessary here (cc @galessiorob ), but looks like it could be useful for developers/linguists/etc interested in your work. Just had a brief look at the paper, and it looks OK! Nothing to comment here, good job! >Automated tests: Are there automated tests or manual steps described so that the functionality of the software can be verified? The paper talks about `helayo` and also about the `matrix-editor`, the web UI. This requirement for automated tests is still missing in `matrix-editor`. The `helayo` program has the dependencies managed via a package manager, and unit tests. But `matrix-editor` doesn't have either (https://github.com/chchch/sanskrit-alignment/issues/3). I thought about checking that option since the paper appears to be about `helayo`, but I think `matrix-editor` is really important & useful to readers and users. The `matrix-editor` is mentioned and has figures of the `matrix-editor` in the paper, and the source code is in the same repository (it's almost as if it's part of `helayo`?) So I think that option remains unchecked. Not sure if that's a blocker for the publication @galessiorob ? If not my review is complete. >Installation instructions: Is there a clearly-stated list of dependencies? Ideally these should be handled with an automated package management solution. `matrix-editor` has the dependencies files copied in the repository, instead of using an automated package management solution, but I noted that in the above it said "ideally", that's why I checked that :+1: but would be better to use yarn/npm/cnd/etc instead, IMO. Bruno","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/openjournals/joss-reviews/issues/comments/1047194855/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
